title: Coach Tan Kim Her's Strategy Behind Hari-Arjun Pairing
indian_angle: Indian fans will be interested in how national doubles coach Tan Kim Her is developing new pairings like Hariharan and Arjun, following the success of Satwik-Chirag.
summary: Doubles coach Tan Kim Her discusses his approach to forming new successful Indian men's doubles pairs, drawing parallels with the strategy used for Satwik-Chirag, now focusing on Hariharan and Arjun.

title: PV Sindhu Needs More Self-Belief to Overcome An Se-young
indian_angle: This analysis focuses on what India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu needs to do to overcome her strong rival An Se-young, a key challenge for her on the world tour.
summary: An article discusses the mental and strategic adjustments PV Sindhu needs to make to consistently challenge and defeat world-beater An Se-young, emphasizing the importance of self-belief.

title: Ayush Shetty Upsets Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang
indian_angle: Young Indian talent Ayush Shetty delivered a significant upset by defeating China's World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang, showcasing his potential on the international stage.
summary: Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty secured a notable victory over Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang, demonstrating a strong performance against a higher-ranked opponent.

title: India Invites Bids for BWF World Championships 2026 in New Delhi
indian_angle: India is set to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in New Delhi, highlighting the country's growing prominence in global badminton.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for the BWF World Championships 2026, confirming New Delhi as the host city from August 17-23, 2026.

title: Sindhu, Arjun-Hariharan Advance to Indonesia Open Round 2
indian_angle: India's top shuttlers PV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair Arjun and Hariharan secured their spots in the second round of the Indonesia Open.
summary: PV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair of Arjun and Hariharan Amsakarunan successfully navigated their opening matches at the Indonesia Open, progressing to Round 2.

title: Grassroot Coaches Development Program Launched in Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: This program aims to strengthen badminton at the grassroots level across India by developing new coaching talent in key regional centers.
summary: BAI has announced a Coaches Development Program for grassroots level coaches, to be held at NCE Guwahati & Raipur from June 12th to 21st, 2026.
